region_of_origin: United States (Dunkin' Donuts brand); served in Singapore
food_subgroup: Hot coffee-based beverages; espresso milk drinks with chocolate flavouring

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general 'coffee beverage' class (FOODON_03301116); no specific mocha latte subclass exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 AN000A covers coffee and coffee substitutes as a category. No specific mocha latte code exists; closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 34731007 represents coffee as a substance. No specific mocha latte concept exists; closest parent used.

serving.natural_language_portion: One medium hot mocha swirl latte served in a 355 ml (12 fl oz) cup, as typically offered at Dunkin' Donuts Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Beverages, coffee, mocha, prepared with whole milk — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy / Foundation Foods
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact Dunkin' Donuts Hot Mocha Swirl Latte entry exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or ICMR-NIN. Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central data for a café mocha prepared with whole milk and chocolate syrup (NDB No. 14209 and related entries), normalised to per-100 g basis. Serving size estimated at 355 g (medium 12 fl oz cup, density approximated at ~1.03 g/ml for milk-based coffee beverage). Values represent a typical whole-milk mocha latte with chocolate syrup; actual Dunkin' product may vary slightly.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; café latte / milk-based coffee beverage analogous estimate
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for a Dunkin' Donuts Hot Mocha Swirl Latte. GI estimated at 41 based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for café latte / milk-based coffee beverages with added sugar/chocolate syrup. Whole milk has a GI of approximately 31–41; the addition of mocha syrup (sucrose-based) raises the blended GI modestly. A composite GI of 41 is used as a conservative analogous estimate. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (41 × 30.2) / 100 ≈ 12.4, rounded to 12.5. Carbohydrate per 355 g serving = 8.5 × 3.55 = 30.2 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Moderate sodium content (~170 mg per serving) and caffeine
hypertension.guidance: A single serving provides approximately 170 mg sodium, which is within acceptable limits for most hypertensive individuals, but caffeine from espresso may cause a transient blood pressure rise; individuals with poorly controlled hypertension should limit intake to one serving per day and monitor their response.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Added sugar from mocha syrup contributing approximately 27.7 g sugar per serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: The mocha syrup contributes significant added sugar (~27.7 g per 355 ml serving), which can elevate postprandial blood glucose; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should request a sugar-free or reduced-syrup version and account for the carbohydrate load (~30 g per serving) within their daily carbohydrate budget.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from whole milk (~4.6 g per serving) and moderate sugar content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The saturated fat content (~4.6 g per serving from whole milk) is moderate and within typical daily limits for cardiovascular health; choosing a reduced-fat milk option and omitting whipped cream topping would meaningfully lower saturated fat and caloric intake for individuals managing cardiovascular disease risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Hot Mocha Swirl Latte is a hot espresso-based coffee beverage combining a shot or double shot of espresso with steamed whole milk and mocha (chocolate-flavoured) syrup, typically finished with a swirl of chocolate sauce and optionally topped with whipped cream. It is sold at Dunkin' Donuts outlets in Singapore, including the Funan mall location. The drink delivers a rich, bittersweet chocolate-coffee flavour profile with a creamy, velvety texture from the steamed milk. Nutritionally, a medium 355 ml serving provides approximately 220 kcal, 10 g fat, 30 g carbohydrate (predominantly from added sugar in the mocha syrup), and 10 g protein from the milk. The glycemic index is estimated at 41 (low), though the glycemic load per serving is moderate at approximately 12.5 due to the volume of added sugar. Clinically, the beverage is of moderate concern for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes owing to its added sugar content, and of low concern for hypertension and cardiovascular disease when consumed in moderation. It is vegetarian, halal-friendly, and contains dairy and possible soy allergens from the mocha syrup. Common user queries include calorie count, sugar content, suitability for diabetics, and whether a sugar-free version is available.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a Dunkin Donuts mocha latte?
Is the hot mocha latte high in sugar?
Can a diabetic drink a mocha latte?
What is the caffeine content of a mocha swirl latte?
Is Dunkin mocha latte halal in Singapore?
How much protein is in a mocha latte?
Is there a sugar-free mocha latte at Dunkin?
What milk is used in the mocha swirl latte?
How many carbs in a hot mocha latte?
Is the mocha latte suitable for lactose-intolerant people?

confidence_reason: No exact Dunkin' Donuts Hot Mocha Swirl Latte entry exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, or USDA Branded Foods with full nutrient disclosure. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central data for a café mocha prepared with whole milk and chocolate syrup, normalised to per-100 g. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for milk-based coffee beverages. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central (café mocha / whole-milk latte reference entries) and normalised to per-100 g. Actual Dunkin' Donuts Singapore product formulation may differ. 2. trans_fat_g is 0.0; confirmed as effectively zero/trace for a milk-based espresso beverage per USDA data — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. 3. Serving size of 355 g (medium 12 fl oz) is an estimate based on standard Dunkin' Donuts medium cup volume with density approximated at ~1.03 g/ml. 4. GI of 41 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this specific product was found in the University of Sydney GI Database. 5. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, and all micronutrients are estimated from USDA reference data for whole-milk café mocha; values are plausible but should be verified against the actual product if Dunkin' Singapore publishes full nutrition data. 6. Halal status is assumed based on Dunkin' Donuts Singapore's general halal certification; kosher status is not confirmed. 7. Multilingual names for Chinese, Malay, Tamil, and Hindi are provided as this product is served in Singapore. 8. The food_group has been reclassified from 'Mixed' (input) to 'Beverages' as this is a hot coffee drink.
